revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: 「'''新田神社'''（にったじんじゃ）は、[[神奈川県]][[川崎市]][[川崎区]][[渡田]]2ｰ14ｰ8にある[[神社]]。[[新田義貞]]、[…」
revision.text:
'''新田神社'''（にったじんじゃ）は、[[神奈川県]][[川崎市]][[川崎区]][[渡田]]2ｰ14ｰ8にある[[神社]]。[[新田義貞]]、[[天照大神]]、[[大国主神]]、[[厳島媛神]]を[[祭神]]として祀る<ref name="新田神社">{{Cite news |url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|title=新田神社（にったじんじゃ）|newspaper=|publisher=[[神社本庁]]神奈川県神社庁|date=|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20160812220635/http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|archivedate=2016-08-12}}</ref>。
== 歴史 == 新田義貞が[[1338年]]に[[越前国]]で戦死した時に家臣の亘新左衛門源早勝が新田義貞の差添の名剣、七ツ入子の名鏡、錦の陣羽織をこの地に持ち帰って供養したところ、土地の人も新田義貞を追慕し、[[1375年]]に一本松の下に差添の名剣、七ツ入子の名鏡、錦の陣羽織を埋納し、[[廟]]を設営して新田大明神として鎮守とする<ref name="新田神社"/>。その後、[[1873年]]に村社に列格、その後、幣帛料供進神社。[[太平洋戦争]]により建物が焼失したが、[[1956年]]に社務所、[[1963年]]に社殿、[[1968年]]に神楽殿を再建し、[[1991年]]に再度社務所を建築した<ref name="新田神社"/>。
== 脚注 == <references />
{{DEFAULTSORT:につたしんしや}} [[Category:神奈川県の神社]] [[Category:神奈川県の旧県社]] [[Category:川崎市の歴史]] [[Category:川崎市の建築物]] [[Category:新田氏]]

'''新田神社'''（にったじんじゃ）は、[[神奈川県]][[川崎市]][[川崎区]][[渡田]]2ｰ14ｰ8にある[[神社]]。[[新田義貞]]、[[天照大神]]、[[大国主神]]、[[厳島媛神]]を[[祭神]]として祀る<ref name="新田神社">{{Cite news |url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|title=新田神社（にったじんじゃ）|newspaper=|publisher=[[神社本庁]]神奈川県神社庁|date=|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20160812220635/http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|archivedate=2016-08-12}}</ref>。
== 歴史 == 新田義貞が[[1338年]]に[[越前国]]で戦死した時に家臣の亘新左衛門源早勝が新田義貞の差添の名剣、七ツ入子の名鏡、錦の陣羽織をこの地に持ち帰って供養したところ、土地の人も新田義貞を追慕し、[[1375年]]に一本松の下に差添の名剣、七ツ入子の名鏡、錦の陣羽織を埋納し、[[廟]]を設営して新田大明神として鎮守とする<ref name="新田神社"/>。その後、[[1873年]]に村社に列格、その後、幣帛料供進神社。[[太平洋戦争]]により建物が焼失したが、[[1956年]]に社務所、[[1963年]]に社殿、[[1968年]]に神楽殿を再建し、[[1991年]]に再度社務所を建築した<ref name="新田神社"/>。
== 脚注 == <references /> {{coord|35|31|14.9|N|139|42|22.8|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{DEFAULTSORT:につたしんしや}} [[Category:神奈川県の神社]] [[Category:神奈川県の旧県社]] [[Category:川崎市の歴史]] [[Category:川崎市の建築物]] [[Category:新田氏]]

'''新田神社'''（にったじんじゃ）は、[[神奈川県]][[川崎市]][[川崎区]][[渡田]]2ｰ14ｰ8にある[[神社]]。[[新田義貞]]、[[天照大神]]、[[大国主神]]、[[厳島媛神]]を[[祭神]]として祀る<ref name="新田神社">{{Cite news |url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|title=新田神社（にったじんじゃ）|newspaper=|publisher=[[神社本庁]]神奈川県神社庁|date=|archiveurl=http://web.archive.org/web/20160812220635/http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|archivedate=2016-08-12}}</ref>。
== 歴史 == 新田義貞が[[1338年]]に[[越前国]]で戦死した時に家臣の亘新左衛門源早勝が新田義貞の差添の名剣、七ツ入子の名鏡、錦の陣羽織をこの地に持ち帰って供養したところ、土地の人も新田義貞を追慕し、[[1375年]]に一本松の下に差添の名剣、七ツ入子の名鏡、錦の陣羽織を埋納し、[[廟]]を設営して新田大明神として鎮守とする<ref name="新田神社"/>。その後、[[1873年]]に村社に列格、その後、幣帛料供進神社。[[太平洋戦争]]により建物が焼失したが、[[1956年]]に社務所、[[1963年]]に社殿、[[1968年]]に神楽殿を再建し、[[1991年]]に再度社務所を建築した<ref name="新田神社"/>。

{{DEFAULTSORT:につたしんしや}} [[Category:神奈川県の旧村社]] [[Category:川崎市の神社]] [[Category:川崎区の歴史]] [[Category:川崎区の建築物]] [[Category:新田氏|社につた かわさき]]

{{神社 |名称 = 新田神社 |画像=[[File:Nitta Jinja (Kawasaki).JPG|240px]] |所在地 = [[神奈川県]][[川崎市]][[川崎区]][[渡田]]2-14-8 |地図 = |祭神 = [[新田義貞]]、[[天照大神]]、[[大国主|大国主神]]、[[厳島媛神]] |社格 = 村社、幣帛料供進神社 |創建 = [[文中]]4年（[[1375年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''新田神社'''（にったじんじゃ）は、[[神奈川県]][[川崎市]][[川崎区]][[渡田]]2-14-8にある[[神社]]。[[新田義貞]]、[[天照大神]]、[[大国主|大国主神]]、[[厳島媛神]]を[[祭神]]として祀る<ref name="新田神社">{{Cite news |url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|title=新田神社（にったじんじゃ）|newspaper=|publisher=[[神社本庁]]神奈川県神社庁|date=|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20160812220635/http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|archivedate=2016-08-12}}</ref>。
== 歴史 == 新田義貞が[[1338年]]に[[越前国]]で戦死した時に家臣の亘新左衛門源早勝が新田義貞の差添の名剣、七ツ入子の名鏡、錦の陣羽織をこの地に持ち帰って供養したところ、土地の人も新田義貞を追慕し、[[1375年]]に一本松の下に差添の名剣、七ツ入子の名鏡、錦の陣羽織を埋納し、[[廟]]を設営して新田大明神として鎮守とする<ref name="新田神社"/>。その後、[[1873年]]に村社に列格、その後、幣帛料供進神社。[[太平洋戦争]]により建物が焼失したが、[[1956年]]に社務所、[[1963年]]に社殿、[[1968年]]に神楽殿を再建し、[[1991年]]に再度社務所を建築した<ref name="新田神社"/>。
== 脚注 == <references />
== 関連項目 == * [[稲毛神社]]

{{Shrine | Name = Nitta Shrine | Image = [[File:Nitta Jinja (Kawasaki).JPG|250px]]
Torii and worship hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|31|14.8|N|139|42|23|E}}}} | Location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Kawasaki City]], [[Kawasaki Ward]], [[Watarida]] 2-14-8 | Coordinates = {{Coord|35|31|14.8|N|139|42|23|E|display=inline,title}} | Deity = [[Nitta Yoshisada]], [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], [[Ichikishimahime]] | Shrine rank = Village shrine, Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine | Established = [[Bunchū]] 4 (1375) }} '''Nitta Shrine''' (新田神社, Nitta Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at Watarida 2-14-8 in [[Kawasaki Ward]], [[Kawasaki City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Nitta Yoshisada]], [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], and [[Ichikishimahime]].{{Cite news |url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|title=Nitta Shrine (Nitta Jinja)|publisher=[[Association of Shinto Shrines]] Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20160812220635/http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|archivedate=2016-08-12}} == History == When [[Nitta Yoshisada]] died in battle in [[1338]] in [[Echizen Province]], his retainer Watarida Shinzemon Minamoto Hayakatsu brought Yoshisada's treasured sword, the Nanatsu Iriko mirror, and the brocade war surcoat back to this area. To honor Yoshisada, local people buried these items under a pine tree and erected a shrine, calling it "Nitta Daimyōjin" as a local guardian. In [[1873]], the shrine was designated a village shrine and later became a Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine. Although the buildings were destroyed during [[World War II]], the shrine was rebuilt in stages: the office in [[1956]], the main shrine in [[1963]], the kagura hall in [[1968]], and a new office in [[1991]]. == References == == Related Topics == * [[Inage Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nitta Shrine}} [[Category:Former village shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Kawasaki City]] [[Category:History of Kawasaki Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Kawasaki Ward]] [[Category:Nitta Yoshisada]] [[Category:Nitta clan shrines|Nitta Kawasaki]]

It enshrines [[Nitta Yoshisada]], [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], and [[Ichikishimahime]].{{Cite news |url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|title=Nitta Shrine (Nitta Jinja)|publisher=[[Association of Shinto Shrines]] Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20160812220635/http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|archivedate=2016-08-12}} == History == When [[Nitta Yoshisada]] died in battle in [[1338]] in [[Echizen Province]], his retainer Watarida Shinzemon Minamoto Hayakatsu brought Yoshisada's treasured sword, the Nanatsu Iriko mirror, and the brocade war surcoat back to this area. To honor Yoshisada, local people buried these items under a pine tree and erected a shrine, calling it "Nitta Daimyōjin" as a local guardian. In [[1873]], the shrine was designated a village shrine and later became a Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine. Although the buildings were destroyed during [[World War II]], the shrine was rebuilt in stages: the office in [[1956]], the main shrine in [[1963]], the kagura hall in [[1968]], and a new office in [[1991]]. == References == == Related Topics == * [[Inage Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nitta Shrine}} [[Category:Former village shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Kawasaki City]] [[Category:History of Kawasaki Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Kawasaki Ward]] [[Category:Nitta Yoshisada]] [[Category:Nitta clan shrines|Nitta Kawasaki]] [[Category:Kk]][[ja:新田神社 (川崎市)]]

{{Shrine | Name = Nitta Shrine | Image = [[File:Nitta Jinja (Kawasaki).JPG|250px]]
Torii and worship hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|31|14.8|N|139|42|23|E}}}} | Location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Kawasaki City]], [[Kawasaki Ward]], [[Watarida]] 2-14-8 | Coordinates = {{Coord|35|31|14.8|N|139|42|23|E|display=inline,title}} | Deity = [[Nitta Yoshisada]], [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], [[Ichikishimahime]] | Shrine rank = Village shrine, Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine | Established = [[Bunchū]] 4 (1375) }} '''Nitta Shrine''' (新田神社, Nitta Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at Watarida 2-14-8 in [[Kawasaki Ward]], [[Kawasaki City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. In [[1873]], the shrine was designated a village shrine and later became a Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine. Although the buildings were destroyed during [[World War II]], the shrine was rebuilt in stages: the office in [[1956]], the main shrine in [[1963]], the kagura hall in [[1968]], and a new office in [[1991]]. == References == == Related Topics == * [[Inage Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nitta Shrine}} [[Category:Former village shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Kawasaki City]] [[Category:History of Kawasaki Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Kawasaki Ward]] [[Category:Nitta Yoshisada]] [[Category:Nitta clan shrines|Nitta Kawasaki]] [[Category:Kk]] [[Category:new pages]][[category:original pages]] [[ja:新田神社 (川崎市)]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Nitta Shrine | image = [[File:Nitta Jinja (Kawasaki).JPG|250px]]
Torii and worship hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|31|14.8|N|139|42|23|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Kawasaki City]], [[Kawasaki Ward]], [[Watarida]] 2-14-8 | Coordinates = {{Coord|35|31|14.8|N|139|42|23|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Nitta Yoshisada]], [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], [[Ichikishimahime]] | Shrine rank = Village shrine, Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine | Established = [[Bunchū]] 4 (1375) }} '''Nitta Shrine''' (新田神社, Nitta Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at Watarida 2-14-8 in [[Kawasaki Ward]], [[Kawasaki City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Nitta Yoshisada]], [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], and [[Ichikishimahime]].{{Cite news |url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|title=Nitta Shrine (Nitta Jinja)|publisher=[[Association of Shinto Shrines]] Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20160812220635/http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|archivedate=2016-08-12}} == History == When [[Nitta Yoshisada]] died in battle in [[1338]] in [[Echizen Province]], his retainer Watarida Shinzemon Minamoto Hayakatsu brought Yoshisada's treasured sword, the Nanatsu Iriko mirror, and the brocade war surcoat back to this area. To honor Yoshisada, local people buried these items under a pine tree and erected a shrine, calling it "Nitta Daimyōjin" as a local guardian. In [[1873]], the shrine was designated a village shrine and later became a Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine. Although the buildings were destroyed during [[World War II]], the shrine was rebuilt in stages: the office in [[1956]], the main shrine in [[1963]], the kagura hall in [[1968]], and a new office in [[1991]]. == References == == Related Topics == * [[Inage Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nitta Shrine}} [[Category:Link complete]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Shrines in Kawasaki City]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:History of Kawasaki Ward]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Buildings in Kawasaki Ward]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Nitta Yoshisada]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Nitta clan shrines|Nitta Kawasaki]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[ja:新田神社 (川崎市)]]

Torii and worship hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|31|14.8|N|139|42|23|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Kawasaki City]], [[Kawasaki Ward]], [[Watarida]] 2-14-8 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|31|14.8|N|139|42|23|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Nitta Yoshisada]], [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], [[Ichikishimahime]] | Shrine rank = Village shrine, Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine | Established = [[Bunchū]] 4 (1375) }} '''Nitta Shrine''' (新田神社, Nitta Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at Watarida 2-14-8 in [[Kawasaki Ward]], [[Kawasaki City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Nitta Yoshisada]], [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], and [[Ichikishimahime]].{{Cite news |url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|title=Nitta Shrine (Nitta Jinja)|publisher=[[Association of Shinto Shrines]] Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20160812220635/http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|archivedate=2016-08-12}} == History == When [[Nitta Yoshisada]] died in battle in [[1338]] in [[Echizen Province]], his retainer Watarida Shinzemon Minamoto Hayakatsu brought Yoshisada's treasured sword, the Nanatsu Iriko mirror, and the brocade war surcoat back to this area. To honor Yoshisada, local people buried these items under a pine tree and erected a shrine, calling it "Nitta Daimyōjin" as a local guardian. In [[1873]], the shrine was designated a village shrine and later became a Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine. Although the buildings were destroyed during [[World War II]], the shrine was rebuilt in stages: the office in [[1956]], the main shrine in [[1963]], the kagura hall in [[1968]], and a new office in [[1991]]. == References == == Related Topics == * [[Inage Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nitta Shrine}} [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing {{Shrine | name = Nitta Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nitta Jinja (Kawasaki).JPG|250px]]
Torii and worship hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|31|14.8|N|139|42|23|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Kawasaki City]], [[Kawasaki Ward]], [[Watarida]] 2-14-8 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|31|14.8|N|139|42|23|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Nitta Yoshisada]], [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], [[Ichikishimahime]] | Shrine rank = Village shrine, Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine | Established = [[Bunchū]] 4 (1375) }} '''Nitta Shrine''' (新田神社, Nitta Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at Watarida 2-14-8 in [[Kawasaki Ward]], [[Kawasaki City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Nitta Yoshisada]], [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], and [[Ichikishimahime]].{{Cite news |url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|title=Nitta Shrine (Nitta Jinja)|publisher=[[Association of Shinto Shrines]] Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20160812220635/http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|archivedate=2016-08-12}} == History == When [[Nitta Yoshisada]] died in battle in [[1338]] in [[Echizen Province]], his retainer Watarida Shinzemon Minamoto Hayakatsu brought Yoshisada's treasured sword, the Nanatsu Iriko mirror, and the brocade war surcoat back to this area. To honor Yoshisada, local people buried these items under a pine tree and erected a shrine, calling it "Nitta Daimyōjin" as a local guardian. In [[1873]], the shrine was designated a village shrine and later became a Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine. Although the buildings were destroyed during [[World War II]], the shrine was rebuilt in stages: the office in [[1956]], the main shrine in [[1963]], the kagura hall in [[1968]], and a new office in [[1991]]. == References == == Related Topics == * [[Inage Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nitta Shrine}} [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] Torii and worship hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|31|14.8|N|139|42|23|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Kawasaki City]], [[Kawasaki Ward]], [[Watarida]] 2-14-8 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|31|14.8|N|139|42|23|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Nitta Yoshisada]], [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], [[Ichikishimahime]] | Shrine rank = Village shrine, Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine | Established = [[Bunchū]] 4 (1375) }} '''Nitta Shrine''' (新田神社, Nitta Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at Watarida 2-14-8 in [[Kawasaki Ward]], [[Kawasaki City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Nitta Yoshisada]], [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], and [[Ichikishimahime]].{{Cite news |url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|title=Nitta Shrine (Nitta Jinja)|publisher=[[Association of Shinto Shrines]] Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20160812220635/http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|archivedate=2016-08-12}} == History == When [[Nitta Yoshisada]] died in battle in [[1338]] in [[Echizen Province]], his retainer Watarida Shinzemon Minamoto Hayakatsu brought Yoshisada's treasured sword, the Nanatsu Iriko mirror, and the brocade war surcoat back to this area. To honor Yoshisada, local people buried these items under a pine tree and erected a shrine, calling it "Nitta Daimyōjin" as a local guardian. In [[1873]], the shrine was designated a village shrine and later became a Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine. Although the buildings were destroyed during [[World War II]], the shrine was rebuilt in stages: the office in [[1956]], the main shrine in [[1963]], the kagura hall in [[1968]], and a new office in [[1991]]. == References == == Related Topics == * [[Inage Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nitta Shrine}} [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Nitta Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nitta Jinja (Kawasaki).JPG|250px]]
Torii and worship hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|31|14.8|N|139|42|23|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Kawasaki City]], [[Kawasaki Ward]], [[Watarida]] 2-14-8 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|31|14.8|N|139|42|23|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Nitta Yoshisada]], [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], [[Ichikishimahime]] | Shrine rank = Village shrine, Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine | Established = [[Bunchū]] 4 (1375) }} '''Nitta Shrine''' (新田神社, Nitta Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at Watarida 2-14-8 in [[Kawasaki Ward]], [[Kawasaki City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Nitta Yoshisada]], [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], and [[Ichikishimahime]].{{Cite news |url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|title=Nitta Shrine (Nitta Jinja)|publisher=[[Association of Shinto Shrines]] Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20160812220635/http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|archivedate=2016-08-12}} == History == When [[Nitta Yoshisada]] died in battle in [[1338]] in [[Echizen Province]], his retainer Watarida Shinzemon Minamoto Hayakatsu brought Yoshisada's treasured sword, the Nanatsu Iriko mirror, and the brocade war surcoat back to this area. To honor Yoshisada, local people buried these items under a pine tree and erected a shrine, calling it "Nitta Daimyōjin" as a local guardian. In [[1873]], the shrine was designated a village shrine and later became a Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine. Although the buildings were destroyed during [[World War II]], the shrine was rebuilt in stages: the office in [[1956]], the main shrine in [[1963]], the kagura hall in [[1968]], and a new office in [[1991]]. == References == == Related Topics == * [[Inage Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nitta Shrine}} [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Nitta Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nitta Jinja (Kawasaki).JPG|250px]]
Torii and worship hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|31|14.8|N|139|42|23|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Kawasaki City]], [[Kawasaki Ward]], [[Watarida]] 2-14-8 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|31|14.8|N|139|42|23|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Nitta Yoshisada]], [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], [[Ichikishimahime]] | Shrine rank = Village shrine, Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine | Established = [[Bunchū]] 4 (1375) }} '''Nitta Shrine''' (新田神社, Nitta Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at Watarida 2-14-8 in [[Kawasaki Ward]], [[Kawasaki City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Nitta Yoshisada]], [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], and [[Ichikishimahime]].{{Cite news |url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|title=Nitta Shrine (Nitta Jinja)|publisher=[[Association of Shinto Shrines]] Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20160812220635/http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|archivedate=2016-08-12}} == History == When [[Nitta Yoshisada]] died in battle in [[1338]] in [[Echizen Province]], his retainer Watarida Shinzemon Minamoto Hayakatsu brought Yoshisada's treasured sword, the Nanatsu Iriko mirror, and the brocade war surcoat back to this area. To honor Yoshisada, local people buried these items under a pine tree and erected a shrine, calling it "Nitta Daimyōjin" as a local guardian. In [[1873]], the shrine was designated a village shrine and later became a Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine. Although the buildings were destroyed during [[World War II]], the shrine was rebuilt in stages: the office in [[1956]], the main shrine in [[1963]], the kagura hall in [[1968]], and a new office in [[1991]]. == References == == Related Topics == * [[Inage Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nitta Shrine}} [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Nitta Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nitta Jinja (Kawasaki).JPG|250px]]
Torii and worship hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|31|14.8|N|139|42|23|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Kawasaki City]], [[Kawasaki Ward]], [[Watarida]] 2-14-8 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|31|14.8|N|139|42|23|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Nitta Yoshisada]], [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], [[Ichikishimahime]] | Shrine rank = Village shrine, Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine | Established = [[Bunchū]] 4 (1375) }} '''Nitta Shrine''' (新田神社, Nitta Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at Watarida 2-14-8 in [[Kawasaki Ward]], [[Kawasaki City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Nitta Yoshisada]], [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], and [[Ichikishimahime]].{{Cite news |url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|title=Nitta Shrine (Nitta Jinja)|publisher=[[Association of Shinto Shrines]] Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20160812220635/http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|archivedate=2016-08-12}} == History == When [[Nitta Yoshisada]] died in battle in [[1338]] in [[Echizen Province]], his retainer Watarida Shinzemon Minamoto Hayakatsu brought Yoshisada's treasured sword, the Nanatsu Iriko mirror, and the brocade war surcoat back to this area. To honor Yoshisada, local people buried these items under a pine tree and erected a shrine, calling it "Nitta Daimyōjin" as a local guardian. In [[1873]], the shrine was designated a village shrine and later became a Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine. Although the buildings were destroyed during [[World War II]], the shrine was rebuilt in stages: the office in [[1956]], the main shrine in [[1963]], the kagura hall in [[1968]], and a new office in [[1991]]. == References == == Related Topics == * [[Inage Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nitta Shrine}} [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Nitta Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nitta Jinja (Kawasaki).JPG|250px]]
Torii and worship hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|31|14.8|N|139|42|23|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Kawasaki City]], [[Kawasaki Ward]], [[Watarida]] 2-14-8 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|31|14.8|N|139|42|23|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Nitta Yoshisada]], [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], [[Ichikishimahime]] | Shrine rank = Village shrine, Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine | Established = [[Bunchū]] 4 (1375) }} '''Nitta Shrine''' (新田神社, Nitta Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at Watarida 2-14-8 in [[Kawasaki Ward]], [[Kawasaki City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Nitta Yoshisada]], [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], and [[Ichikishimahime]].{{Cite news |url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|title=Nitta Shrine (Nitta Jinja)|publisher=[[Association of Shinto Shrines]] Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20160812220635/http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|archivedate=2016-08-12}} == History == When [[Nitta Yoshisada]] died in battle in [[1338]] in [[Echizen Province]], his retainer Watarida Shinzemon Minamoto Hayakatsu brought Yoshisada's treasured sword, the Nanatsu Iriko mirror, and the brocade war surcoat back to this area. To honor Yoshisada, local people buried these items under a pine tree and erected a shrine, calling it "Nitta Daimyōjin" as a local guardian. In [[1873]], the shrine was designated a village shrine and later became a Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine. Although the buildings were destroyed during [[World War II]], the shrine was rebuilt in stages: the office in [[1956]], the main shrine in [[1963]], the kagura hall in [[1968]], and a new office in [[1991]]. == References == == See Also == * [[Inage Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nitta Shrine}} [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Bumping to try to fix the sexagenary stuff]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Nitta Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nitta Jinja (Kawasaki).JPG|250px]]
Torii and worship hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|31|14.8|N|139|42|23|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Kawasaki City]], [[Kawasaki Ward]], [[Watarida]] 2-14-8 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|31|14.8|N|139|42|23|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Nitta Yoshisada]], [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], [[Ichikishimahime]] | Shrine rank = Village shrine, Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine | Established = [[Bunchū]] 4 (1375) }} '''Nitta Shrine''' (新田神社, Nitta Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at Watarida 2-14-8 in [[Kawasaki Ward]], [[Kawasaki City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Nitta Yoshisada]], [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], and [[Ichikishimahime]].{{Cite news |url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|title=Nitta Shrine (Nitta Jinja)|publisher=[[Association of Shinto Shrines]] Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20160812220635/http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|archivedate=2016-08-12}} == History == When [[Nitta Yoshisada]] died in battle in [[1338]] in [[Echizen Province]], his retainer Watarida Shinzemon Minamoto Hayakatsu brought Yoshisada's treasured sword, the Nanatsu Iriko mirror, and the brocade war surcoat back to this area. To honor Yoshisada, local people buried these items under a pine tree and erected a shrine, calling it "Nitta Daimyōjin" as a local guardian. In [[1873]], the shrine was designated a village shrine and later became a Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine. Although the buildings were destroyed during [[World War II]], the shrine was rebuilt in stages: the office in [[1956]], the main shrine in [[1963]], the kagura hall in [[1968]], and a new office in [[1991]]. == References == == See Also == * [[Inage Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nitta Shrine}} [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nitta Shrine (Kawasaki City)]][[Category:qq]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Nitta Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nitta Jinja (Kawasaki).JPG|250px]]
Torii and worship hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|31|14.8|N|139|42|23|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Kawasaki City]], [[Kawasaki Ward]], [[Watarida]] 2-14-8 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|31|14.8|N|139|42|23|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Nitta Yoshisada]], [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], [[Ichikishimahime]] | Shrine rank = Village shrine, Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine | Established = [[Bunchū]] 4 (1375) }} '''Nitta Shrine''' (新田神社, Nitta Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at Watarida 2-14-8 in [[Kawasaki Ward]], [[Kawasaki City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Nitta Yoshisada]], [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], and [[Ichikishimahime]].{{Cite news |url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|title=Nitta Shrine (Nitta Jinja)|publisher=[[Association of Shinto Shrines]] Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20160812220635/http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|archivedate=2016-08-12}} == History == When [[Nitta Yoshisada]] died in battle in [[1338]] in [[Echizen Province]], his retainer Watarida Shinzemon Minamoto Hayakatsu brought Yoshisada's treasured sword, the Nanatsu Iriko mirror, and the brocade war surcoat back to this area. To honor Yoshisada, local people buried these items under a pine tree and erected a shrine, calling it "Nitta Daimyōjin" as a local guardian. In [[1873]], the shrine was designated a village shrine and later became a Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine. Although the buildings were destroyed during [[World War II]], the shrine was rebuilt in stages: the office in [[1956]], the main shrine in [[1963]], the kagura hall in [[1968]], and a new office in [[1991]]. == References == == See Also == * [[Inage Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nitta Shrine}} [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nitta Shrine (Kawasaki City)]][[Category:qq]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Nitta Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nitta Jinja (Kawasaki).JPG|250px]]
Torii and worship hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|31|14.8|N|139|42|23|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Kawasaki City]], [[Kawasaki Ward]], [[Watarida]] 2-14-8 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|31|14.8|N|139|42|23|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Nitta Yoshisada]], [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], [[Ichikishimahime]] | Shrine rank = Village shrine, Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine | Established = [[Bunchū]] 4 (1375) }} '''Nitta Shrine''' (新田神社, Nitta Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at Watarida 2-14-8 in [[Kawasaki Ward]], [[Kawasaki City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Nitta Yoshisada]], [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], and [[Ichikishimahime]].{{Cite news |url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|title=Nitta Shrine (Nitta Jinja)|publisher=[[Association of Shinto Shrines]] Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20160812220635/http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|archivedate=2016-08-12}} == History == When [[Nitta Yoshisada]] died in battle in [[1338]] in [[Echizen Province]], his retainer Watarida Shinzemon Minamoto Hayakatsu brought Yoshisada's treasured sword, the Nanatsu Iriko mirror, and the brocade war surcoat back to this area. To honor Yoshisada, local people buried these items under a pine tree and erected a shrine, calling it "Nitta Daimyōjin" as a local guardian. In [[1873]], the shrine was designated a village shrine and later became a Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine. Although the buildings were destroyed during [[World War II]], the shrine was rebuilt in stages: the office in [[1956]], the main shrine in [[1963]], the kagura hall in [[1968]], and a new office in [[1991]]. == References == == See Also == * [[Inage Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nitta Shrine}} [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nitta Shrine (Kawasaki City)]][[Category:qq]] Torii and worship hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|31|14.8|N|139|42|23|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Kawasaki City]], [[Kawasaki Ward]], [[Watarida]] 2-14-8 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|31|14.8|N|139|42|23|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Nitta Yoshisada]], [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], [[Ichikishimahime]] | Shrine rank = Village shrine, Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine | Established = [[Bunchū]] 4 (1375) }} '''Nitta Shrine''' (新田神社, Nitta Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at Watarida 2-14-8 in [[Kawasaki Ward]], [[Kawasaki City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Nitta Yoshisada]], [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], and [[Ichikishimahime]].{{Cite news |url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|title=Nitta Shrine (Nitta Jinja)|publisher=[[Association of Shinto Shrines]] Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20160812220635/http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|archivedate=2016-08-12}} == History == When [[Nitta Yoshisada]] died in battle in [[1338]] in [[Echizen Province]], his retainer Watarida Shinzemon Minamoto Hayakatsu brought Yoshisada's treasured sword, the Nanatsu Iriko mirror, and the brocade war surcoat back to this area. To honor Yoshisada, local people buried these items under a pine tree and erected a shrine, calling it "Nitta Daimyōjin" as a local guardian. In [[1873]], the shrine was designated a village shrine and later became a Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine. Although the buildings were destroyed during [[World War II]], the shrine was rebuilt in stages: the office in [[1956]], the main shrine in [[1963]], the kagura hall in [[1968]], and a new office in [[1991]]. == References == == See Also == * [[Inage Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nitta Shrine}} [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nitta Shrine (Kawasaki City)]][[Category:qq]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Nitta Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nitta Jinja (Kawasaki).JPG|250px]]
Torii and worship hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|31|14.8|N|139|42|23|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Kawasaki City]], [[Kawasaki Ward]], [[Watarida]] 2-14-8 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|31|14.8|N|139|42|23|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Nitta Yoshisada]], [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], [[Ichikishimahime]] | Shrine rank = Village shrine, Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine | Established = [[Bunchū]] 4 (1375) }} '''Nitta Shrine''' (新田神社, Nitta Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at Watarida 2-14-8 in [[Kawasaki Ward]], [[Kawasaki City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Nitta Yoshisada]], [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], and [[Ichikishimahime]].{{Cite news |url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|title=Nitta Shrine (Nitta Jinja)|publisher=[[Association of Shinto Shrines]] Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20160812220635/http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|archivedate=2016-08-12}} == History == When [[Nitta Yoshisada]] died in battle in [[1338]] in [[Echizen Province]], his retainer Watarida Shinzemon Minamoto Hayakatsu brought Yoshisada's treasured sword, the Nanatsu Iriko mirror, and the brocade war surcoat back to this area. To honor Yoshisada, local people buried these items under a pine tree and erected a shrine, calling it "Nitta Daimyōjin" as a local guardian. In [[1873]], the shrine was designated a village shrine and later became a Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine. Although the buildings were destroyed during [[World War II]], the shrine was rebuilt in stages: the office in [[1956]], the main shrine in [[1963]], the kagura hall in [[1968]], and a new office in [[1991]]. == References == == See Also == * [[Inage Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nitta Shrine}} [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nitta Shrine (Kawasaki City)]][[Category:qq]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Nitta Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nitta Jinja (Kawasaki).JPG|250px]]
Torii and worship hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|31|14.8|N|139|42|23|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Kawasaki City]], [[Kawasaki Ward]], [[Watarida]] 2-14-8 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|31|14.8|N|139|42|23|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Nitta Yoshisada]], [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], [[Ichikishimahime]] | Shrine rank = Village shrine, Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine | Established = [[Bunchū]] 4 (1375) }} '''Nitta Shrine''' (新田神社, Nitta Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at Watarida 2-14-8 in [[Kawasaki Ward]], [[Kawasaki City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Nitta Yoshisada]], [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], and [[Ichikishimahime]].{{Cite news |url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|title=Nitta Shrine (Nitta Jinja)|publisher=[[Association of Shinto Shrines]] Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20160812220635/http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|archivedate=2016-08-12}} == History == When [[Nitta Yoshisada]] died in battle in [[1338]] in [[Echizen Province]], his retainer Watarida Shinzemon Minamoto Hayakatsu brought Yoshisada's treasured sword, the Nanatsu Iriko mirror, and the brocade war surcoat back to this area. To honor Yoshisada, local people buried these items under a pine tree and erected a shrine, calling it "Nitta Daimyōjin" as a local guardian. In [[1873]], the shrine was designated a village shrine and later became a Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine. Although the buildings were destroyed during [[World War II]], the shrine was rebuilt in stages: the office in [[1956]], the main shrine in [[1963]], the kagura hall in [[1968]], and a new office in [[1991]]. == References == == See Also == * [[Inage Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nitta Shrine}} [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nitta Shrine (Kawasaki City)]][[Category:qq]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Nitta Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nitta Jinja (Kawasaki).JPG|250px]]
Torii and worship hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|31|14.8|N|139|42|23|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Kawasaki City]], [[Kawasaki Ward]], [[Watarida]] 2-14-8 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|31|14.8|N|139|42|23|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Nitta Yoshisada]], [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], [[Ichikishimahime]] | Shrine rank = Village shrine, Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine | Established = [[Bunchū]] 4 (1375) }} '''Nitta Shrine''' (新田神社, Nitta Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at Watarida 2-14-8 in [[Kawasaki Ward]], [[Kawasaki City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Nitta Yoshisada]], [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], and [[Ichikishimahime]].{{Cite news |url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|title=Nitta Shrine (Nitta Jinja)|publisher=[[Association of Shinto Shrines]] Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20160812220635/http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|archivedate=2016-08-12}} == History == When [[Nitta Yoshisada]] died in battle in [[1338]] in [[Echizen Province]], his retainer Watarida Shinzemon Minamoto Hayakatsu brought Yoshisada's treasured sword, the Nanatsu Iriko mirror, and the brocade war surcoat back to this area. To honor Yoshisada, local people buried these items under a pine tree and erected a shrine, calling it "Nitta Daimyōjin" as a local guardian. In [[1873]], the shrine was designated a village shrine and later became a Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine. Although the buildings were destroyed during [[World War II]], the shrine was rebuilt in stages: the office in [[1956]], the main shrine in [[1963]], the kagura hall in [[1968]], and a new office in [[1991]]. == References == == See Also == * [[Inage Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nitta Shrine}} [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nitta Shrine (Kawasaki City)]][[Category:11]] To honor Yoshisada, local people buried these items under a pine tree and erected a shrine, calling it "Nitta Daimyōjin" as a local guardian. In [[1873]], the shrine was designated a village shrine and later became a Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine. Although the buildings were destroyed during [[World War II]], the shrine was rebuilt in stages: the office in [[1956]], the main shrine in [[1963]], the kagura hall in [[1968]], and a new office in {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Nitta Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nitta Jinja (Kawasaki).JPG|250px]]
Torii and worship hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|31|14.8|N|139|42|23|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Kawasaki City]], [[Kawasaki Ward]], [[Watarida]] 2-14-8 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|31|14.8|N|139|42|23|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Nitta Yoshisada]], [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], [[Ichikishimahime]] | Shrine rank = Village shrine, Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine | Established = [[Bunchū]] 4 (1375) }} '''Nitta Shrine''' (新田神社, Nitta Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at Watarida 2-14-8 in [[Kawasaki Ward]], [[Kawasaki City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Nitta Yoshisada]], [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], and [[Ichikishimahime]].{{Cite news |url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|title=Nitta Shrine (Nitta Jinja)|publisher=[[Association of Shinto Shrines]] Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20160812220635/http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|archivedate=2016-08-12}} == History == When [[Nitta Yoshisada]] died in battle in [[1338]] in [[Echizen Province]], his retainer Watarida Shinzemon Minamoto Hayakatsu brought Yoshisada's treasured sword, the Nanatsu Iriko mirror, and the brocade war surcoat back to this area. To honor Yoshisada, local people buried these items under a pine tree and erected a shrine, calling it "Nitta Daimyōjin" as a local guardian. In [[1873]], the shrine was designated a village shrine and later became a Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine. Although the buildings were destroyed during [[World War II]], the shrine was rebuilt in stages: the office in [[1956]], the main shrine in [[1963]], the kagura hall in [[1968]], and a new office in [[1991]]. == References == == See Also == * [[Inage Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nitta Shrine}} [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nitta Shrine (Kawasaki City)]][[Category:11]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Nitta Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nitta Jinja (Kawasaki).JPG|250px]]
Torii and worship hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|31|14.8|N|139|42|23|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Kawasaki City]], [[Kawasaki Ward]], [[Watarida]] 2-14-8 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|31|14.8|N|139|42|23|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Nitta Yoshisada]], [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], [[Ichikishimahime]] | Shrine rank = Village shrine, Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine | Established = [[Bunchū]] 4 (1375) }} '''Nitta Shrine''' (新田神社, Nitta Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at Watarida 2-14-8 in [[Kawasaki Ward]], [[Kawasaki City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Nitta Yoshisada]], [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], and [[Ichikishimahime]].{{Cite news |url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|title=Nitta Shrine (Nitta Jinja)|publisher=[[Association of Shinto Shrines]] Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20160812220635/http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|archivedate=2016-08-12}} == History == When [[Nitta Yoshisada]] died in battle in [[1338]] in [[Echizen Province]], his retainer Watarida Shinzemon Minamoto Hayakatsu brought Yoshisada's treasured sword, the Nanatsu Iriko mirror, and the brocade war surcoat back to this area. To honor Yoshisada, local people buried these items under a pine tree and erected a shrine, calling it "Nitta Daimyōjin" as a local guardian. In [[1873]], the shrine was designated a village shrine and later became a Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine. Although the buildings were destroyed during [[World War II]], the shrine was rebuilt in stages: the office in [[1956]], the main shrine in [[1963]], the kagura hall in [[1968]], and a new office in [[1991]]. == References == == See Also == * [[Inage Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nitta Shrine}} [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nitta Shrine (Kawasaki City)]][[Category:11]] To honor Yoshisada, local people buried these items under a pine tree and erected a shrine, calling it "Nitta Daimyōjin" as a local guardian. In [[1873]], the shrine was designated a village shrine and later became a Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine. Although the buildings were destroyed during [[World War II]], the shrine was rebuilt in stages: the office in [[1956]], the main shrine in [[1963]], the kagura hall in [[1968]], and a new office in {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Nitta Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nitta Jinja (Kawasaki).JPG|250px]]
Torii and worship hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|31|14.8|N|139|42|23|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Kawasaki City]], [[Kawasaki Ward]], [[Watarida]] 2-14-8 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|31|14.8|N|139|42|23|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Nitta Yoshisada]], [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], [[Ichikishimahime]] | Shrine rank = Village shrine, Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine | Established = [[Bunchū]] 4 (1375) }} '''Nitta Shrine''' (新田神社, Nitta Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at Watarida 2-14-8 in [[Kawasaki Ward]], [[Kawasaki City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Nitta Yoshisada]], [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], and [[Ichikishimahime]].{{Cite news |url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|title=Nitta Shrine (Nitta Jinja)|publisher=[[Association of Shinto Shrines]] Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20160812220635/http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|archivedate=2016-08-12}} == History == When [[Nitta Yoshisada]] died in battle in [[1338]] in [[Echizen Province]], his retainer Watarida Shinzemon Minamoto Hayakatsu brought Yoshisada's treasured sword, the Nanatsu Iriko mirror, and the brocade war surcoat back to this area. To honor Yoshisada, local people buried these items under a pine tree and erected a shrine, calling it "Nitta Daimyōjin" as a local guardian. In [[1873]], the shrine was designated a village shrine and later became a Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine. Although the buildings were destroyed during [[World War II]], the shrine was rebuilt in stages: the office in [[1956]], the main shrine in [[1963]], the kagura hall in [[1968]], and a new office in [[1991]]. == References == == See Also == * [[Inage Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nitta Shrine}} [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nitta Shrine (Kawasaki City)]][[Category:11]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Nitta Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nitta Jinja (Kawasaki).JPG|250px]]
Torii and worship hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|31|14.8|N|139|42|23|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Kawasaki City]], [[Kawasaki Ward]], [[Watarida]] 2-14-8 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|31|14.8|N|139|42|23|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Nitta Yoshisada]], [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], [[Ichikishimahime]] | Shrine rank = Village shrine, Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine | Established = [[Bunchū]] 4 (1375) }} '''Nitta Shrine''' (新田神社, Nitta Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at Watarida 2-14-8 in [[Kawasaki Ward]], [[Kawasaki City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Nitta Yoshisada]], [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], and [[Ichikishimahime]].{{Cite news |url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|title=Nitta Shrine (Nitta Jinja)|publisher=[[Association of Shinto Shrines]] Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20160812220635/http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|archivedate=2016-08-12}} == History == When [[Nitta Yoshisada]] died in battle in [[1338]] in [[Echizen Province]], his retainer Watarida Shinzemon Minamoto Hayakatsu brought Yoshisada's treasured sword, the Nanatsu Iriko mirror, and the brocade war surcoat back to this area. To honor Yoshisada, local people buried these items under a pine tree and erected a shrine, calling it "Nitta Daimyōjin" as a local guardian. In [[1873]], the shrine was designated a village shrine and later became a Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine. Although the buildings were destroyed during [[World War II]], the shrine was rebuilt in stages: the office in [[1956]], the main shrine in [[1963]], the kagura hall in [[1968]], and a new office in [[1991]]. == References == == See Also == * [[Inage Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nitta Shrine}} [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nitta Shrine (Kawasaki City)]][[Category:11]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Nitta Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nitta Jinja (Kawasaki).JPG|250px]]
Torii and worship hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|31|14.8|N|139|42|23|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Kawasaki City]], [[Kawasaki Ward]], [[Watarida]] 2-14-8 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|31|14.8|N|139|42|23|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Nitta Yoshisada]], [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], [[Ichikishimahime]] | Shrine rank = Village shrine, Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine | Established = [[Bunchū]] 4 (1375) }} '''Nitta Shrine''' (新田神社, Nitta Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at Watarida 2-14-8 in [[Kawasaki Ward]], [[Kawasaki City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Nitta Yoshisada]], [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], and [[Ichikishimahime]].{{Cite news |url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|title=Nitta Shrine (Nitta Jinja)|publisher=[[Association of Shinto Shrines]] Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20160812220635/http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|archivedate=2016-08-12}} == History == When [[Nitta Yoshisada]] died in battle in [[1338]] in [[Echizen Province]], his retainer Watarida Shinzemon Minamoto Hayakatsu brought Yoshisada's treasured sword, the Nanatsu Iriko mirror, and the brocade war surcoat back to this area. To honor Yoshisada, local people buried these items under a pine tree and erected a shrine, calling it "Nitta Daimyōjin" as a local guardian. In [[1873]], the shrine was designated a village shrine and later became a Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine. Although the buildings were destroyed during [[World War II]], the shrine was rebuilt in stages: the office in [[1956]], the main shrine in [[1963]], the kagura hall in [[1968]], and a new office in [[1991]]. == References == == See Also == * [[Inage Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nitta Shrine}} [[Category:completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 4]][[sn:Nitta Shrine (Kawasaki City)]][[Category:completion {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Nitta Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nitta Jinja (Kawasaki).JPG|250px]]
Torii and worship hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|31|14.8|N|139|42|23|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Kawasaki City]], [[Kawasaki Ward]], [[Watarida]] 2-14-8 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|31|14.8|N|139|42|23|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Nitta Yoshisada]], [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], [[Ichikishimahime]] | Shrine rank = Village shrine, Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine | Established = [[Bunchū]] 4 (1375) }} '''Nitta Shrine''' (新田神社, Nitta Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at Watarida 2-14-8 in [[Kawasaki Ward]], [[Kawasaki City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Nitta Yoshisada]], [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], and [[Ichikishimahime]].{{Cite news |url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|title=Nitta Shrine (Nitta Jinja)|publisher=[[Association of Shinto Shrines]] Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20160812220635/http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|archivedate=2016-08-12}} == History == When [[Nitta Yoshisada]] died in battle in [[1338]] in [[Echizen Province]], his retainer Watarida Shinzemon Minamoto Hayakatsu brought Yoshisada's treasured sword, the Nanatsu Iriko mirror, and the brocade war surcoat back to this area. To honor Yoshisada, local people buried these items under a pine tree and erected a shrine, calling it "Nitta Daimyōjin" as a local guardian. In [[1873]], the shrine was designated a village shrine and later became a Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine. Although the buildings were destroyed during [[World War II]], the shrine was rebuilt in stages: the office in [[1956]], the main shrine in [[1963]], the kagura hall in [[1968]], and a new office in [[1991]]. == References == == See Also == * [[Inage Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nitta Shrine}} [[Category:completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category: tier 4]][[sn:Nitta Shrine (Kawasaki {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Nitta Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nitta Jinja (Kawasaki).JPG|250px]]
Torii and worship hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|31|14.8|N|139|42|23|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Kawasaki City]], [[Kawasaki Ward]], [[Watarida]] 2-14-8 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|31|14.8|N|139|42|23|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Nitta Yoshisada]], [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], [[Ichikishimahime]] | Shrine rank = Village shrine, Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine | Established = [[Bunchū]] 4 (1375) }} '''Nitta Shrine''' (新田神社, Nitta Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at Watarida 2-14-8 in [[Kawasaki Ward]], [[Kawasaki City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Nitta Yoshisada]], [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], and [[Ichikishimahime]].{{Cite news |url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|title=Nitta Shrine (Nitta Jinja)|publisher=[[Association of Shinto Shrines]] Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20160812220635/http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|archivedate=2016-08-12}} == History == When [[Nitta Yoshisada]] died in battle in [[1338]] in [[Echizen Province]], his retainer Watarida Shinzemon Minamoto Hayakatsu brought Yoshisada's treasured sword, the Nanatsu Iriko mirror, and the brocade war surcoat back to this area. To honor Yoshisada, local people buried these items under a pine tree and erected a shrine, calling it "Nitta Daimyōjin" as a local guardian. In [[1873]], the shrine was designated a village shrine and later became a Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine. Although the buildings were destroyed during [[World War II]], the shrine was rebuilt in stages: the office in [[1956]], the main shrine in [[1963]], the kagura hall in [[1968]], and a new office in [[1991]]. == References == == See Also == * [[Inage Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nitta Shrine}} [[Category:completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category: tier 4]][[sn:Nitta Shrine (Kawasaki City)]][[Category:completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category: tier 4]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nitta Shrine (Kawasaki City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Nitta Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nitta Jinja (Kawasaki).JPG|250px]]
Torii and worship hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|31|14.8|N|139|42|23|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Kawasaki City]], [[Kawasaki Ward]], [[Watarida]] 2-14-8 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|31|14.8|N|139|42|23|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Nitta Yoshisada]], [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], [[Ichikishimahime]] | Shrine rank = Village shrine, Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine | Established = [[Bunchū]] 4 (1375) }} '''Nitta Shrine''' (新田神社, Nitta Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at Watarida 2-14-8 in [[Kawasaki Ward]], [[Kawasaki City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Nitta Yoshisada]], [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], and [[Ichikishimahime]].{{Cite news |url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|title=Nitta Shrine (Nitta Jinja)|publisher=[[Association of Shinto Shrines]] Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20160812220635/http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|archivedate=2016-08-12}} == History == When [[Nitta Yoshisada]] died in battle in [[1338]] in [[Echizen Province]], his retainer Watarida Shinzemon Minamoto Hayakatsu brought Yoshisada's treasured sword, the Nanatsu Iriko mirror, and the brocade war surcoat back to this area. To honor Yoshisada, local people buried these items under a pine tree and erected a shrine, calling it "Nitta Daimyōjin" as a local guardian. In [[1873]], the shrine was designated a village shrine and later became a Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine. Although the buildings were destroyed during [[World War II]], the shrine was rebuilt in stages: the office in [[1956]], the main shrine in [[1963]], the kagura hall in [[1968]], and a new office in [[1991]]. == References == == See Also == * [[Inage Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nitta Shrine}} [[Category:completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 6]][[sn:Nitta Shrine (Kawasaki City)]][[Category:completion {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Nitta Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nitta Jinja (Kawasaki).JPG|250px]]

It enshrines [[Nitta Yoshisada]], [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], and [[Ichikishimahime]].{{Cite news |url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|title=Nitta Shrine (Nitta Jinja)|publisher=[[Association of Shinto Shrines]] Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20160812220635/http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|archivedate=2016-08-12}} == History == When [[Nitta Yoshisada]] died in battle in [[1338]] in [[Echizen Province]], his retainer Watarida Shinzemon Minamoto Hayakatsu brought Yoshisada's treasured sword, the Nanatsu Iriko mirror, and the brocade war surcoat back to this area. To honor Yoshisada, local people buried these items under a pine tree and erected a shrine, calling it "Nitta Daimyōjin" as a local guardian. In [[1873]], the shrine was designated a village shrine and later became a Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine. Although the buildings were destroyed during [[World War II]], the shrine was rebuilt in stages: the office in [[1956]], the main shrine in [[1963]], the kagura hall in [[1968]], and a new office in [[1991]]. == References == == See Also == * [[Inage Shrine]] {{draft categories| [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category: tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Nitta Shrine (Kawasaki City)]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Kawasaki City]] [[Category:History of Kawasaki Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Kawasaki Ward]] [[Category:Nitta Yoshisada]] [[Category:Nitta clan shrines|Nitta Kawasaki]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[sn:Nitta Shrine (Kawasaki City)]] [[ja:新田神社 (川崎市)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nitta Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nitta Shrine (Kawasaki City)}}

Torii and worship hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|31|14.8|N|139|42|23|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Kawasaki City]], [[Kawasaki Ward]], [[Watarida]] 2-14-8 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|31|14.8|N|139|42|23|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Nitta Yoshisada]], [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], [[Ichikishimahime]] | Shrine rank = Village shrine, Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine | Established = [[Bunchū]] 4 (1375) }} '''Nitta Shrine''' (新田神社, Nitta Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at Watarida 2-14-8 in [[Kawasaki Ward]], [[Kawasaki City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Nitta Yoshisada]], [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], and [[Ichikishimahime]].{{Cite news |url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|title=Nitta Shrine (Nitta Jinja)|publisher=[[Association of Shinto Shrines]] Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20160812220635/http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|archivedate=2016-08-12}} == History == When [[Nitta Yoshisada]] died in battle in [[1338]] in [[Echizen Province]], his retainer Watarida Shinzemon Minamoto Hayakatsu brought Yoshisada's treasured sword, the Nanatsu Iriko mirror, and the brocade war surcoat back to this area. To honor Yoshisada, local people buried these items under a pine tree and erected a shrine, calling it "Nitta Daimyōjin" as a local guardian. In [[1873]], the shrine was designated a village shrine and later became a Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine. Although the buildings were destroyed during [[World War II]], the shrine was rebuilt in stages: the office in [[1956]], the main shrine in [[1963]], the kagura hall in [[1968]], and a new office in [[1991]]. == References == == See Also == * [[Inage Shrine]] {{draft categories| [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Nitta Shrine (Kawasaki City)]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Kawasaki City]] [[Category:History of Kawasaki Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Kawasaki Ward]] [[Category:Nitta Yoshisada]] [[Category:Nitta clan shrines|Nitta Kawasaki]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[sn:Nitta Shrine (Kawasaki City)]] [[ja:新田神社 (川崎市)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nitta Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nitta Shrine (Kawasaki City)}}

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 新田神社 |画像=[[File:Nitta Jinja (Kawasaki).JPG|250px]]
鳥居と拝殿 {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|31|14.8|N|139|42|23|E}}}} |所在地 = [[神奈川県]][[川崎市]][[川崎区]][[渡田]]2-14-8 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|31|14.8|N|139|42|23|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[新田義貞]]、[[天照大神]]、[[大国主|大国主神]]、[[イチキシマヒメ|厳島媛神]] |社格 = 村社、幣帛料供進神社 |創建 = [[文中]]4年（[[1375年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''新田神社'''（にったじんじゃ）は、[[神奈川県]][[川崎市]][[川崎区]][[渡田]]2-14-8にある[[神社]]。[[新田義貞]]、[[天照大神]]、[[大国主|大国主神]]、[[イチキシマヒメ|厳島媛神]]を[[祭神]]として祀る{{Cite news |url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|title=新田神社（にったじんじゃ）|newspaper=|publisher=[[神社本庁]]神奈川県神社庁|date=|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20160812220635/http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|archivedate=2016-08-12}}。 == 歴史 == 新田義貞が[[1338年]]に[[越前国]]で戦死した時に家臣の亘新左衛門源早勝が新田義貞の差添の名剣、七ツ入子の名鏡、錦の陣羽織をこの地に持ち帰って供養したところ、土地の人も新田義貞を追慕し、[[1375年]]に一本松の下に差添の名剣、七ツ入子の名鏡、錦の陣羽織を埋納し、[[廟]]を設営して新田大明神として鎮守とする。その後、[[1873年]]に村社に列格、その後、幣帛料供進神社。[[太平洋戦争]]により建物が焼失したが、[[1956年]]に社務所、[[1963年]]に社殿、[[1968年]]に神楽殿を再建し、[[1991年]]に再度社務所を建築した。 == 脚注 == == 関連項目 == * [[稲毛神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:につたしんしや}} [[Category:神奈川県の旧村社]] [[Category:川崎市の神社]] [[Category:川崎区の歴史]] [[Category:川崎区の建築物]] [[Category:新田義貞]] [[Category:新田氏|社につた かわさき]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[新田神社 (川崎市)]] to [[Nitta Shrine (Kawasaki City)]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 新田神社 |画像=[[File:Nitta Jinja (Kawasaki).JPG|250px]]
鳥居と拝殿 {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|31|14.8|N|139|42|23|E}}}} |所在地 = [[神奈川県]][[川崎市]][[川崎区]][[渡田]]2-14-8 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|31|14.8|N|139|42|23|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[新田義貞]]、[[天照大神]]、[[大国主|大国主神]]、[[イチキシマヒメ|厳島媛神]] |社格 = 村社、幣帛料供進神社 |創建 = [[文中]]4年（[[1375年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''新田神社'''（にったじんじゃ）は、[[神奈川県]][[川崎市]][[川崎区]][[渡田]]2-14-8にある[[神社]]。[[新田義貞]]、[[天照大神]]、[[大国主|大国主神]]、[[イチキシマヒメ|厳島媛神]]を[[祭神]]として祀る{{Cite news |url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|title=新田神社（にったじんじゃ）|newspaper=|publisher=[[神社本庁]]神奈川県神社庁|date=|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20160812220635/http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|archivedate=2016-08-12}}。 == 歴史 == 新田義貞が[[1338年]]に[[越前国]]で戦死した時に家臣の亘新左衛門源早勝が新田義貞の差添の名剣、七ツ入子の名鏡、錦の陣羽織をこの地に持ち帰って供養したところ、土地の人も新田義貞を追慕し、[[1375年]]に一本松の下に差添の名剣、七ツ入子の名鏡、錦の陣羽織を埋納し、[[廟]]を設営して新田大明神として鎮守とする。その後、[[1873年]]に村社に列格、その後、幣帛料供進神社。[[太平洋戦争]]により建物が焼失したが、[[1956年]]に社務所、[[1963年]]に社殿、[[1968年]]に神楽殿を再建し、[[1991年]]に再度社務所を建築した。 == 脚注 == == 関連項目 == * [[稲毛神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:につたしんしや}} [[Category:神奈川県の旧村社]] [[Category:川崎市の神社]] [[Category:川崎区の歴史]] [[Category:川崎区の建築物]] [[Category:新田義貞]] [[Category:新田氏|社につた かわさき]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Nitta Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nitta Jinja (Kawasaki).JPG|250px]]
Torii and worship hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|31|14.8|N|139|42|23|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Kawasaki City]], [[Kawasaki Ward]], [[Watarida]] 2-14-8 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|31|14.8|N|139|42|23|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Nitta Yoshisada]], [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], [[Ichikishimahime]] | Shrine Rank = Village shrine, Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine | Established = [[Bunchū]] 4 (1375) }} '''Nitta Shrine''' (新田神社, Nitta Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at Watarida 2-14-8 in [[Kawasaki Ward]], [[Kawasaki City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Nitta Yoshisada]], [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], and [[Ichikishimahime]].{{Cite news |url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|title=Nitta Shrine (Nitta Jinja)|publisher=[[Association of Shinto Shrines]] Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20160812220635/http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|archivedate=2016-08-12}} == History == When [[Nitta Yoshisada]] died in battle in [[1338]] in [[Echizen Province]], his retainer Watarida Shinzemon Minamoto Hayakatsu brought Yoshisada's treasured sword, the Nanatsu Iriko mirror, and the brocade war surcoat back to this area. To honor Yoshisada, local people buried these items under a pine tree and erected a shrine, calling it "Nitta Daimyōjin" as a local guardian. In [[1873]], the shrine was designated a village shrine and later became a Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine. Although the buildings were destroyed during [[World War II]], the shrine was rebuilt in stages: the office in [[1956]], the main shrine in [[1963]], the kagura hall in [[1968]], and a new office in [[1991]]. == References == == See Also == * [[Inage Shrine]] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Nitta Shrine (Kawasaki City)]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Kawasaki City]] [[Category:History of Kawasaki Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Kawasaki Ward]] [[Category:Nitta Yoshisada]] [[Category:Nitta clan shrines|Nitta Kawasaki]] }} [[sn:Nitta Shrine (Kawasaki City)]] [[ja:新田神社 (川崎市)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nitta Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nitta Shrine (Kawasaki City)}} {{translated page|ja|新田神社 (川崎市)|version=93927201|comment=Imported full ja history}}

In [[1873]], the shrine was designated a village shrine and later became a Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine. Although the buildings were destroyed during [[World War II]], the shrine was rebuilt in stages: the office in [[1956]], the main shrine in [[1963]], the kagura hall in [[1968]], and a new office in [[1991]]. == References == == See Also == * [[Inage Shrine]] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Nitta Shrine (Kawasaki City)]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Kawasaki City]] [[Category:History of Kawasaki Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Kawasaki Ward]] [[Category:Nitta Yoshisada]] [[Category:Nitta clan shrines|Nitta Kawasaki]] }} [[sn:Nitta Shrine (Kawasaki City)]] [[ja:新田神社 (川崎市)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nitta Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nitta Shrine (Kawasaki City)}} {{translated page|ja|新田神社 (川崎市)|version=93927201|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Torii and worship hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|31|14.8|N|139|42|23|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Kawasaki City]], [[Kawasaki Ward]], [[Watarida]] 2-14-8 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|31|14.8|N|139|42|23|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Nitta Yoshisada]], [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], [[Ichikishima-hime]] | Shrine Rank = Village shrine, Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine | Established = [[Bunchū]] 4 (1375) }} '''Nitta Shrine''' (新田神社, Nitta Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at Watarida 2-14-8 in [[Kawasaki Ward]], [[Kawasaki City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. revision.comment: Text replacement - "Former Village shrines" to "Former Village Shrines"
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Nitta Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nitta Jinja (Kawasaki).JPG|250px]]
Torii and worship hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|31|14.8|N|139|42|23|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Kawasaki City]], [[Kawasaki Ward]], [[Watarida]] 2-14-8 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|31|14.8|N|139|42|23|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Nitta Yoshisada]], [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], [[Ichikishima-hime]] | Shrine Rank = Village shrine, Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine | Established = [[Bunchū]] 4 (1375) }} '''Nitta Shrine''' (新田神社, Nitta Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at Watarida 2-14-8 in [[Kawasaki Ward]], [[Kawasaki City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Nitta Yoshisada]], [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], and [[Ichikishima-hime]].{{Cite news |url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|title=Nitta Shrine (Nitta Jinja)|publisher=[[Association of Shinto Shrines]] Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20160812220635/http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|archivedate=2016-08-12}} == History == When [[Nitta Yoshisada]] died in battle in [[1338]] in [[Echizen Province]], his retainer Watarida Shinzemon Minamoto Hayakatsu brought Yoshisada's treasured sword, the Nanatsu Iriko mirror, and the brocade war surcoat back to this area. To honor Yoshisada, local people buried these items under a pine tree and erected a shrine, calling it "Nitta Daimyōjin" as a local guardian. In [[1873]], the shrine was designated a village shrine and later became a Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine. Although the buildings were destroyed during [[World War II]], the shrine was rebuilt in stages: the office in [[1956]], the main shrine in [[1963]], the kagura hall in [[1968]], and a new office in [[1991]]. == References == == See Also == * [[Inage Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|新田神社 (川崎市)|version=93927201|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Nitta Shrine (Kawasaki City)]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Kawasaki City]] [[Category:History of Kawasaki Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Kawasaki Ward]] [[Category:Nitta Yoshisada]] [[Category:Nitta clan shrines|Nitta Kawasaki]] }} [[sn:Nitta Shrine (Kawasaki City)]] [[ja:新田神社 (川崎市)]]

It enshrines [[Nitta Yoshisada]], [[Amaterasu]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], and [[Ichikishima-hime]].{{Cite news |url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|title=Nitta Shrine (Nitta Jinja)|publisher=[[Association of Shinto Shrines]] Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20160812220635/http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|archivedate=2016-08-12}} == History == When [[Nitta Yoshisada]] died in battle in [[:en:1338|1338]] in [[Echizen Province]], his retainer Watarida Shinzemon Minamoto Hayakatsu brought Yoshisada's treasured sword, the Nanatsu Iriko mirror, and the brocade war surcoat back to this area. To honor Yoshisada, local people buried these items under a pine tree and erected a shrine, calling it "Nitta Daimyōjin" as a local guardian. In [[1873]], the shrine was designated a village shrine and later became a Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine. Although the buildings were destroyed during [[World War II]], the shrine was rebuilt in stages: the office in [[1956]], the main shrine in [[1963]], the kagura hall in [[1968]], and a new office in [[1991]]. == References == == See Also == * [[Inage Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|新田神社 (川崎市)|version=93927201|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Kawasaki City]] [[Category:History of Kawasaki Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Kawasaki Ward]] }} [[sn:Nitta Shrine (Kawasaki City)]] [[ja:新田神社 (川崎市)]] [[Category:Buildings in Kawasaki-ku, Kawasaki]] [[Category:History of Kawasaki-ku, Kawasaki]] [[Category:Shrines in Kawasaki]] [[Category:Nitta clan]] [[Category:Nitta Yoshisada]] [[Category:神奈川県の旧村社]]

To honor Yoshisada, local people buried these items under a pine tree and erected a shrine, calling it "Nitta Daimyōjin" as a local guardian. In [[:en:1873|1873]], the shrine was designated a village shrine and later became a Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine. Although the buildings were destroyed during [[World War II]], the shrine was rebuilt in stages: the office in [[:en:1956|1956]], the main shrine in [[:en:1963|1963]], the kagura hall in [[:en:1968|1968]], and a new office in [[:en:1991|1991]]. == References == == See Also == * [[Inage Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|新田神社 (川崎市)|version=93927201|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Kawasaki City]] }} [[sn:Nitta Shrine (Kawasaki City)]] [[ja:新田神社 (川崎市)]] [[Category:Buildings in Kawasaki-ku, Kawasaki]] [[Category:History of Kawasaki-ku, Kawasaki]] [[Category:Shrines in Kawasaki]] [[Category:Nitta clan]] [[Category:Nitta Yoshisada]] [[Category:神奈川県の旧村社]]
