It enshrines [[Nitta Yoshisada]], [[Amaterasu]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], and [[Ichikishima-hime]].{{Cite news |url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|title=Nitta Shrine (Nitta Jinja)|publisher=[[Association of Shinto Shrines]] Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20160812220635/http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|archivedate=2016-08-12}} == History == When [[Nitta Yoshisada]] died in battle in [[:en:1338|1338]] in [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]], his retainer Watarida Shinzemon Minamoto Hayakatsu brought Yoshisada's treasured sword, the Nanatsu Iriko mirror, and the brocade war surcoat back to this area. To honor Yoshisada, local people buried these items under a pine tree and erected a shrine, calling it "Nitta Daimyōjin" as a local guardian. In [[:en:1873|1873]], the shrine was designated a village shrine and later became a Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine. Although the buildings were destroyed during [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], the shrine was rebuilt in stages: the office in [[:en:1956|1956]], the main shrine in [[:en:1963|1963]], the kagura hall in [[:en:1968|1968]], and a new office in [[:en:1991|1991]]. == References == == See Also == * [[Inage Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|新田神社 (川崎市)|version=93927201|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] }} [[sn:Nitta Shrine (Kawasaki City)]] [[ja:新田神社 (川崎市)]] [[Category:Buildings in Kawasaki-ku, Kawasaki]] [[Category:History of Kawasaki-ku, Kawasaki]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kawasaki]] [[Category:Nitta clan]] [[Category:Nitta Yoshisada]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

In [[:en:1873|1873]], the shrine was designated a village shrine and later became a Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine. Although the buildings were destroyed during [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], the shrine was rebuilt in stages: the office in [[:en:1956|1956]], the main shrine in [[:en:1963|1963]], the kagura hall in [[:en:1968|1968]], and a new office in [[:en:1991|1991]].<ref name="Nitta Shrine"/>

It enshrines [[Nitta Yoshisada]], [[Amaterasu]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], and [[Ichikishima-hime]].<ref name="Nitta Shrine">{{Cite news |url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|title=Nitta Shrine (Nitta Jinja)|publisher=[[Association of Shinto Shrines]] Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20160812220635/http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|archivedate=2016-08-12}}</ref>
== History == When [[Nitta Yoshisada]] died in battle in [[:en:1338|1338]] in [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]], his retainer Watarida Shinzemon Minamoto Hayakatsu brought Yoshisada's treasured sword, the Nanatsu Iriko mirror, and the brocade war surcoat back to this area. To honor Yoshisada, local people buried these items under a pine tree and erected a shrine, calling it "Nitta Daimyōjin" as a local guardian.<ref name="Nitta Shrine"/>
In [[:en:1873|1873]], the shrine was designated a village shrine and later became a Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine. Although the buildings were destroyed during [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], the shrine was rebuilt in stages: the office in [[:en:1956|1956]], the main shrine in [[:en:1963|1963]], the kagura hall in [[:en:1968|1968]], and a new office in [[:en:1991|1991]].<ref name="Nitta Shrine"/>

It enshrines [[Nitta Yoshisada]], [[Amaterasu]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], and [[Ichikishima-hime]].<ref name="Nitta Shrine">{{Cite news |url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|title=Nitta Shrine (Nitta Jinja)|publisher=[[Association of Shinto Shrines]] Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20160812220635/http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|archivedate=2016-08-12}}</ref>

To honor Yoshisada, local people buried these items under a pine tree and erected a shrine, calling it "Nitta Daimyōjin" as a local guardian.<ref name="Nitta Shrine"/>
In {{ill|1873|en|1873|WD=Q7757|lt=1873}}, the shrine was designated a village shrine and later became a Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine. Although the buildings were destroyed during {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War}}, the shrine was rebuilt in stages: the office in {{ill|1956|en|1956|WD=Q5221|lt=1956}}, the main shrine in {{ill|1963|en|1963|WD=Q2755|lt=1963}}, the kagura hall in {{ill|1968|en|1968|WD=Q2644|lt=1968}}, and a new office in {{ill|1991|en|1991|WD=Q2062|lt=1991}}.<ref name="Nitta Shrine"/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:新田神社 (川崎市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:新田神社 (川崎市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:新田神社 (川崎市)]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Nitta Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Nitta Jinja (Kawasaki).JPG | location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}, [[:en:Kawasaki City|Kawasaki City]], [[Kawasaki Ward]], [[Watarida]] 2-14-8 | coordinates = {{Coord|35.52080556|N|139.70633333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Nitta Yoshisada]], [[Amaterasu]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], [[Ichikishima-hime]] | Shrine Rank = Village shrine, Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine | Established = [[Bunchū]] 4 (1375) | location = {{ill|Kawasaki-ku|WD=Q1204949|1=Kawasaki-ku, Kawasaki}} }}
'''Nitta Shrine''' (新田神社, Nitta Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located at Watarida 2-14-8 in [[Kawasaki Ward]], [[:en:Kawasaki City|Kawasaki City]], {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}. It enshrines [[Nitta Yoshisada]], [[Amaterasu]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], and [[Ichikishima-hime]].<ref name="Nitta Shrine">{{Cite news |url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|title=Nitta Shrine (Nitta Jinja)|publisher=[[Association of Shinto Shrines]] Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20160812220635/http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=3&cd=1201003&scd=&npg=0|archivedate=2016-08-12}}</ref>
== History == When [[Nitta Yoshisada]] died in battle in {{ill|1338|en|1338|WD=Q6044|lt=1338|1=1338}} in {{ill|Echizen Province|en|Echizen Province|WD=Q1047144|lt=Echizen Province|1=Echizen Province}}, his retainer Watarida Shinzemon Minamoto Hayakatsu brought Yoshisada's treasured sword, the Nanatsu Iriko mirror, and the brocade war surcoat back to this area. To honor Yoshisada, local people buried these items under a pine tree and erected a shrine, calling it "Nitta Daimyōjin" as a local guardian.<ref name="Nitta Shrine"/>
In {{ill|1873|en|1873|WD=Q7757|lt=1873|1=1873}}, the shrine was designated a village shrine and later became a Heihaku-ryō Kyojin Shrine. Although the buildings were destroyed during {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War|1=Pacific War}}, the shrine was rebuilt in stages: the office in {{ill|1956|en|1956|WD=Q5221|lt=1956|1=1956}}, the main shrine in {{ill|1963|en|1963|WD=Q2755|lt=1963|1=1963}}, the kagura hall in {{ill|1968|en|1968|WD=Q2644|lt=1968|1=1968}}, and a new office in {{ill|1991|en|1991|WD=Q2062|lt=1991|1=1991}}.<ref name="Nitta Shrine"/>
== References == <references /> == See Also == * [[Inage Shrine]]
